checklist_items.title: Verify that forum requires wallet-based login for RFRV submitters.
checklist_items.description: Check if the Governance Forum requires users to log in using their on-chain wallet to submit RFRVs. This will ensure that identities can be verified securely.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing wallet-based login is required for RFRV submission.

checklist_items.title: Implement input validation for RFRV submissions.
checklist_items.description: Check that all fields in the RFRV submission form are validated to prevent injection attacks and ensure data integrity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum is vulnerable to injection attacks via the RFRV submission form.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust input validation to sanitize all user inputs before processing them.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review of RFRV submission form and backend validation logic.

checklist_items.title: Verify RFRV submission requires wallet-based login
checklist_items.description: Check if the Governance Forum requires a wallet-based login before allowing users to submit RFRVs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot of login screen showing wallet-based authentication requirement. Audit logs showing successful wallet-based logins for RFRV submissions.

checklist_items.title: Verify Rate Limiting Implementation
checklist_items.description: Check that rate limiting mechanisms are in place to mitigate brute-force attacks against authentication endpoints.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system is vulnerable to brute-force attacks if rate limiting is not implemented correctly.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust rate limiting to prevent brute-force attacks on login endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review and testing results demonstrating the effectiveness of rate limiting.

checklist_items.title: Secure Session Management
checklist_items.description: Ensure that secure session management practices are followed, including using strong session IDs, short session timeouts, and HTTPS.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Insecure session management can lead to session hijacking and unauthorized access.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement secure session management practices to protect user sessions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review and penetration testing results confirming secure session management implementation.

checklist_items.title: Input Validation for Login
checklist_items.description: Verify that input validation is implemented to prevent injection attacks (e.g., SQL injection) during the login process.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The login functionality is susceptible to injection attacks without proper input validation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust input validation to prevent injection attacks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review demonstrating secure input handling and penetration testing results.

checklist_items.title: Verify that all RFRV submissions require wallet-based login
checklist_items.description: Check if the Governance Forum requires a logged-in state with wallet-based authentication before allowing RFRV submission.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing wallet-based login is required for RFRV submission.

checklist_items.title: Implement input validation for RFRV data
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all fields in the RFRV submission form are validated to prevent injection attacks and unexpected data.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RFRV submission form is vulnerable to injection attacks due to lack of input validation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust input validation and sanitization for all fields in the RFRV form.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review showing input validation implementation and test cases passing.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit access logs for the Governance Forum
checklist_items.description: Review logs for unauthorized access attempts or suspicious activities related to RFRV submissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Unauthorized access to the Governance Forum could lead to data manipulation or denial-of-service attacks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive logging and regular security audits of the forum.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports showing regular reviews of access logs for suspicious activity.

checklist_items.title: Verify RFRV submission requires wallet-based login

checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.

checklist_items.title: Implement input validation for RFRV fields

checklist_items.assumption_ref: Malicious actors could inject harmful code or manipulate data through the RFRV submission process.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust input validation to sanitize all user inputs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review showing input validation implementation; test cases demonstrating successful rejection of invalid inputs.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit Governance Forum logs for suspicious activity
checklist_items.description: Review logs for unusual patterns of RFRV submissions or access attempts that may indicate malicious activity.

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive logging and regularly audit logs for suspicious activity.

checklist_items.description: Check if all fields in the RFRV submission form are validated to prevent injection attacks (e.g., SQL injection, cross-site scripting).

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust input validation and sanitization for all user inputs related to RFRV submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review demonstrating input validation and sanitization. Test cases showing that invalid inputs are rejected.

checklist_items.title: Verify that forum requires wallet-based login for RFRV submitters
checklist_items.description: Check if the Governance Forum requires users submitting RFRVs to be authenticated via a wallet login, ensuring only genuine users participate.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing wallet-based login requirement for RFRV submissions.

checklist_items.title: Implement input validation for RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Check if there is input validation to prevent malicious or invalid RFRVs from being submitted, and filter out any suspicious data.

checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust input validation and sanitization for all RFRV fields.

checklist_items.title: Verify that all RFRV submissions require wallet-based login
checklist_items.description: Check that the Governance Forum platform enforces wallet-based login before allowing users to submit RFRVs, ensuring only authenticated users can submit proposals.

checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot of login process showing wallet verification, and audit logs of successful RFRV submissions with associated wallet addresses.

checklist_items.title: Implement input validation for RFRV parameters
checklist_items.description: Verify that all input parameters for RFRV submissions (e.g., proposal details, vote options) are sanitized and validated to prevent injection attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum is vulnerable to injection attacks due to insufficient input validation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust input validation mechanisms to sanitize and validate all user inputs
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review demonstrating input validation for RFRV parameters and test cases covering various injection attempts.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit and update access control lists for RFRV administration
checklist_items.description: Check that access to administer or modify RFRVs is restricted to authorized personnel only, with regular reviews of user access.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Unauthorized access to RFRV administration functions could lead to data breaches or manipulation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regularly audit and update access control lists based on the principle of least privilege.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation outlining access control policies, and audit logs of access control modifications.

checklist_items.title: Verify input sanitization for user-provided data
checklist_items.description: Check if all user inputs are properly sanitized to prevent injection attacks such as SQL injection or cross-site scripting (XSS).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User-provided data may contain malicious code.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust input validation and sanitization mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review and testing results demonstrating effective input sanitization.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure authentication and authorization
checklist_items.description: Verify that the system uses strong authentication mechanisms (e.g., multi-factor authentication) and authorization controls to protect sensitive data and functionality.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Unauthorized access to sensitive data is possible.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication and authorization mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of implemented authentication and authorization mechanisms, along with security testing results.

checklist_items.title: Regularly review and update security configurations
checklist_items.description: Check if the system's security configurations are reviewed and updated regularly to address known vulnerabilities and ensure compliance with security best practices.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: System configurations might contain vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regularly review and update security configurations based on security best practices and vulnerability scans.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Documentation of security configuration review and update process, along with records of any changes made.

checklist_items.title: Verify input sanitization for all user inputs
checklist_items.description: Check if all user inputs are properly sanitized to prevent XSS attacks.  This includes validating all data before it's processed by the application.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User inputs are not properly sanitized, leading to vulnerabilities like XSS.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust input validation and sanitization techniques to prevent XSS vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review showing input sanitization, testing demonstrating resistance to XSS attacks.

checklist_items.title: Implement and regularly test authentication mechanisms
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all authentication mechanisms are regularly tested and reviewed for vulnerabilities.  This should include penetration testing to identify weaknesses.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Authentication mechanisms are not sufficiently robust to prevent unauthorized access.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Strengthen authentication mechanisms (e.g., MFA), use secure password storage techniques.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Penetration test reports, security audit confirming authentication robustness.

checklist_items.title: Verify secure coding practices for all components handling sensitive data
checklist_items.description: Review code for vulnerabilities such as SQL injection, cross-site scripting (XSS), and cross-site request forgery (CSRF).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The application contains vulnerabilities that could be exploited by attackers to gain unauthorized access to sensitive data.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement secure coding practices and conduct regular code reviews.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Secure coding guidelines followed; results of code reviews and vulnerability scans.

checklist_items.title: Implement input validation and sanitization for all user inputs
checklist_items.description: Validate all inputs from users to prevent injection attacks such as SQL injection and cross-site scripting (XSS). Sanitize user inputs before using them in the application.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User inputs are not properly sanitized or validated, leading to vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement input validation and sanitization mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review of input validation and sanitization functions; test cases for injection attacks.

checklist_items.title: Regular security audits and penetration testing
checklist_items.description: Regularly conduct security assessments and penetration tests to identify and address vulnerabilities before attackers can exploit them.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The application is vulnerable to attacks due to lack of regular security assessments.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Perform regular security audits and penetration tests.

checklist_items.description: Check that API keys for third-party services are stored securely, using methods such as dedicated secret management solutions and not hardcoding them into the application.

checklist_items.description: Check if API keys are stored using a secure method (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager) and rotated regularly.
